applied_prompts.title: Weight Method

architectural_principles: parameter choices must be traceable to vault notes

applied_prompts.prompt:
Weights should derive from the vault, not from a disconnected config culture.
Parent weight rules define the shared tokenizer and the principle that parameter choices must remain inspectable and local.
Architecture is not exempt from this rule. Model shape, vocab size, and layer counts are architectural decisions and must resolve through the same vault path as training behavior.

applied_prompts.title: Architecture Compilation Contract
applied_prompts.prompt:
This note is the compilation contract for architecture resolution.

Architecture profiles live as child notes under this folder. The compiler walks from the root vault note
through weights, through this note, and into the named profile child. The resolved output is a concrete
set of fields sufficient to build the model — no inference, no defaults hidden in code.

Two profiles currently exist:

- tiny_scratch: the first real scratch experiment, small enough to train on CPU, loss still high
- craig_target: the first serious target profile at Qwen3-0.6B scale, not yet trained

Neither profile imports pretrained weights. Both are random-init. Architecture is not a copy of any
upstream model even if the parameter count resembles one — the vault governs what gets built here,
not any external source.

Adding a new architecture profile means adding a child folder with a _method.md. The compiler picks
up the profile by name. configs/model_architecture.yaml is now a legacy fallback reference only.

applied_prompts.title: Tiny Scratch Architecture Profile

config.description: First real scratch experiment. Small enough to train on CPU in a single session.

applied_prompts.prompt:
This is the first model that actually ran.

~25M parameters. Vocabulary of 96 tokens — the early custom tokenizer, not the Qwen3 reference vocab.
Trained for 2000 steps on the prepared vault corpus (~5.3M tokens). Loss around 8.7 at completion.
Output is not coherent. That is expected. The point of this run was to prove the pipeline, not to
produce a useful model.

Checkpoints exist at artifacts/checkpoints/tiny-qwen3-scratch/. The run also extended further on
the Model-Lab machine (step_11400), but those artifacts are not stored in this repo.

This profile should not be confused with the craig_target profile. They are different shapes serving
different purposes. This one ran. The other has not.

architectural_principles: parameter choices must be traceable to vault notes
resolved_config.description: First real scratch experiment. Small enough to train on CPU in a single session.
